answers.q17: Every vampire can evoke three aspects of the Beast; the monstrous, the seductive, and the competitive. The aura chosen reflects the way the vampire approaches the confrontation. It also determines the Conditions given during the clash. The Monstrous Beast: This represents the Beast-As-Destroyer. Its every instinct is to rip asunder. The monstrous aura inspires a witness to fight or flee. It evokes the most primitive survival instincts in its victims, and causes the Bestial Condition (p. 301). The Seductive Beast: This is the Beast-As-Tempter. It needs hot abandon, and a temporary escape from reality. It inspires the need for immediate, throwaway coupling. It evokes the primal need for instant gratification, and causes the Wanton Condition (p. 307). The Competitive Beast: This is the Beast-As-Alpha. It needs to come out on top, to command, to control. It inspires power plays and dominance games. It evokes the need for a rigid hierarchy, a food chain, and causes the Competitive Condition (p. 302).
answers.q18: Some Kindred choose to grab their Beasts by the reins, and wield that monstrous power. It’s a risky proposition, but one with great rewards. This practice is called riding the wave of frenzy. To ride the wave, spend a Willpower point, and make the normal Resolve + Composure resistance roll. The Willpower point does not add to this roll. Success holds off the frenzy for a turn as if she’d spent Willpower to fight the frenzy. She can continue to do this until she fails, runs out of Willpower, or until she accumulates five successes. Treat any failure on this roll as a dramatic failure. If she reaches five successes, she rides the wave. This functions identically to a normal frenzy, with all normal benefits. However, you may choose the Beast’s desire and specific target at the outset. This desire does not have to align with the provocation. For example, if Victoria risks frenzy because she’s starved, she might choose to kill Dominic instead of feeding. Normally, a frenzied Beast will not force its host to commit diablerie. However, a vampire riding the wave may choose diablerie as the desire. When in frenzy, the vampire becomes stronger, faster, and tougher. Her Beast drives her to feats of terrifying physical prowess. Add her Blood Potency dots to any Strength, Dexterity, or Stamina rolls or resistances. (Do not apply her improved Stamina to her Health levels.) Ignore any wound penalties she should suffer.

answers.q27: Lowest of Dex or Wits + Athletics. You can spend a WP to increase your defense by 2, but only against one attacker. You can apply defense per turn at a cumulative -1 penalty (after the first) for each attack you choose to apply defense to, until you run out. Dodge: At any point before your action, your character can choose to Dodge. Doing so gives up her normal action. When Dodging, double your character’s Defense but do not subtract it from attack rolls. Instead, roll Defense as a dice pool, and subtract each success from the attacker’s successes. If this reduces the attacker’s successes to 0, the attack does no damage. Apply successes from Dodging before adding any weapon bonus.
answers.q28: Bashing, Lethal and Agg. By default, all damage dealt to Kindred is Bashing. Where an ability or power would apply Lethal or Aggravated damage, only the Weapon Rating is upgraded. Thus, a 1L weapon used with Kindred duelling would deliver one Lethal damage + successes Bashing damage. In the case of a 0L weapon, the first success is upgraded to Lethal, with subsequent successes adding Bashing damage.
answers.q29: Fire automatically inflicts aggravated damage per turn of exposure (no attack roll is required). The larger the flame, the more harm that’s inflicted. The hotter the flame, the greater the injury. Mortals take only lethal damage. Each turn, a vampire may turn a number of aggravated damage levels to lethal levels equal to his dots in Resilience. Depends how big. Torch 1, Bonfire 2, Inferno 3 per turn. Heat level also factors. Candle 0 Torch +1 Bunsen burner +2 Chemical fire / molten metal +3
answers.q30: A single point of Vitae heals two points of bashing damage, or one point of lethal damage. A single aggravated wound requires five Vitae and a full day’s sleep. Aggravated damage is the only type of wound that leaves a scar on Kindred. The scar appears natural, unlike the original damage.
answers.q32: When your character resolves a Condition, take a Beat. If a Condition has a natural time limit and then fades away, don’t take a Beat — just waiting the Condition out isn’t enough to count as resolving it.
answers.q33: Any time a vampire withdraws from his outside life in defense of his truer self, he gains a point of Willpower. When committing terrible, damning acts in defense of his personal identity, he regains all his spent Willpower points.

answers.q36: Over time, through great injury, as result of feeding restrictions, or because of starvation, Kindred fall to the sleep of ages: torpor. EDIT also Staking?
answers.q37: Once Removed: This step is for sires and their childer exclusively. Sires and childer enjoy a +3 on all sympathy-based rolls. This level of sympathy has no range limits; sires and childer can feel each other from across the world. Twice Removed: This step is for siblings, grandchilder, and grandsires. Twice-removed Kindred receive a +2 die bonus on all sympathy-based rolls. Sympathy affects any relative on the same continent. Thrice Removed: This step is for cousins, a sire’s siblings, and great grandsires and great grandchilder. Thrice-removed Kindred receive a +1 die bonus on all sympathy-based rolls. Sympathy affects any relative in the same city. Four Times Removed: This final step is for all clanmates. They receive no modifiers for sympathy rolls. Sympathy affects any clanmate within a mile.
answers.q38: If a vampire uses a Discipline on a relative, add the blood sympathy bonus to the roll. Rolls made to enact Crúac sorcery use double this modifier.
answers.q39: Multiply the base duration (based on the vampire’s Humanity dots) by her Blood Potency. After that amount of time in torpor, she can awaken. This isn’t always exact; it could be up to a quarter of the time shorter or longer. As well, any vampire with two more dots of Blood Potency than she has can feed her a point of Vitae to awaken her (with the normal risks of blood bonds and addiction). This can happen at any time. (theres a table on pg 105)
answers.q40: Sympathy comes into effect automatically any time a vampire’s relative enters torpor or suffers Final Death. The feeling washes over the vampire and she senses her relative’s state in vague, often symbolic terms. As well, characters can initiate sympathy to communicate their states of being to their relatives once or twice removed. To do so, they must spend a Willpower point and make a Blood Potency roll. Each success allows them to contact a single relative; the relative must still make the roll to detect sympathy. Any particularly powerful emotion can trigger sympathy, at Storyteller discretion. This is less a foolproof detective tool, and a more dramatic device. Any time a relative suffers a horrible fate, sympathy may cause frenzies.

answers.q46: You buy the level of ritual you can cast up to (so the dot of the "core" power), then you can buy rituals at that level for a set cost. Sacrifice First, then the Request, which is an extended action. Ritualists may roll as many times as the unmodified dice pool. The base time per roll is half an hour, reduced to 15 minutes if the character has more dots in the Ritual Discipline than the dot rating of the ritual being cast. A ritual must be completed in one attempt. Ritualists do not receive any bonus for attempting a ritual having already failed with a near miss; rituals automatically fail if interrupted; and ritualists may not use Defense while casting. Many rituals are also Contested or Resisted, as noted in their descriptions. Unless a Merit that modifies extended actions expressly says that it applies to Sorcery, Crúac or Theban Sorcery rolls are not affected by it. The exception is the Library Merit (p. 121), which applies to Theban Sorcery rituals at the Storyteller’s discretion.

answers.q48: Effect: Your character has fertile feeding grounds, whether officially granted or not. Dots in this Merit represent the ease of hunting in that territory. answers.q51: All characters using conflicting powers enter a contested roll-off, each using a pool of his Blood Potency plus his dots in the Discipline. Devotions use Blood Potency plus the highest rated Discipline among the prerequisites, while blood sorcery uses Blood Potency plus the character’s dots in Crúac or Theban Sorcery. Ties reroll until one player has accrued more success than all others. The effect invoked by that player’s character wins out and resolves as usual, while all others fail. More details are found on pg 125-126.
answers.q53: Conditions represent ways in which the story has affected your character, and what she can do to move past those events. Defence is applied to incoming attacks to reduce damage taken. 
Each time Defence is used in combat, it reduces the pool by 1 (eg. starting with Def 3, applies it once = reduced to 2 for the next use) 
Spending a point of WP increases the Defence by 2 until the end of the turn against 1 attacker.

answers.q17: A vampire may attempt to assert superiority or control over a mortal or another Kindred by flaring up their Beast and lashing out through a predatory aura - the aggressor chooses one of the three aspects of the Beast (Monstrous, Seductive, or Competitive) - the defender may choose to defend or escape - when defending, the defender must choose a bestial aspect of their own and both parties must roll the power attribute associated with the aspect (Monstrous - Strength, Seductive - Presence, and Competitive - Intelligence) plus their Blood Potency - the defender must also pay a point of Willpower if their Blood Potency is lower than the aggressor's - both parties roll their pools and whoever scores the most successes is the victor - on a tie, both Kindred apply the Condition of their aspect to the other (Monstrous - Bestial, Seductive - Wanton, and Competitive - Competitive) - the winner of the clash applies their Condition to their competitor and receive a +2 bonus to pursue the desire of their Beast's aspect - if the defender is mortal, they can not apply a Condition to the vampire through the contest of auras but on succeeding gain a +2 bonus to attack or escape the vampire - the other option, flight, results in an escape or an excuse to leave the situation, leaving the subject of the lashing out seeming weaker or inferior to the aggressor, giving the aggressor an opportunity to pursue them, and they receive the Condition of the aggressor's bestial aspect

answers.q04: A Contested Roll requires both parties to establish a dice pool and roll, comparing the number of successes achieved to determine whether the action was successful or not. The NWOD and VTR v2 books do not state what happens in the case of a tie which has been a point of contention on many forums leading to most player groups simply house ruling based on whatever they feel is most appropriate - but the WOD v1 book states that ties go to the Defender, so it seems most logical that this would carry over to v2 as the rules on Contested rolls are otherwise exactly the same. Resisted rolls requires the active player to establish a dice pool and remove a number of dice from that pool before rolling equal to the relevant Resistance stat of the opponent - usually Resolve, Composure, or Defence depending on the attempted action..
answers.q07: None. Draining blood from a mortal causes 1 Lethal damage per Vitae drained. They also gain either the "Swooning" or "Scarred" condition, depending on whether they were drained seductively or violently. These take effect immediately as far as I can tell.  The errata also says that if you take more than 2 Vitae from a single vessel there is "risk". I presume this means that it will cause have further unspecified story consequences for your character.

answers.q09: When a Kindred drink the Vitae of another Kindred, they may from a Blood Bond. A bond lasts for approximately 1 year from the last drink, and develops in 3 stages, with a tier 3 bond breaking all other lesser bonds the Thrall may have. Each level of bond increases the Regnants social status in the eyes of the Thrall by 1 level, and provides a bonus/penalty equal to the bond level on Social maneuvers and manipulative Discipline rolls. Any attempt by the Thrall to harm the Regnant also constitutes a "breaking point" rolled at humanity 3, 2, or 1 inverse to the level of the Bond.
answers.q11: The relevant Attribute + Skill + the number of dots in the Discipline.
answers.q12: A "Clash of Wills" occurs, requiring an opposed roll using the characters Blood Potency + Dots in the Discipline being used, or the Discipline with the highest prerequisite dots in the case of a Devotion, or dots in Cruac or Theban Sorcery. Ties are rerolled until one party achieves more successes than another, and once rolls are resolved whichever party achieved the most successes gains the benefit of their power activating, while all other powers fail.

answers.q27:
A Characters Defence total is typically made up of the lowest of their Wits and Dex, + the number of dots in Athletics, + dots in Celerity.

A point of Willpower can be spent to raise your Defence by 2 for a round against a single attacker.

You can apply your Defence to any number of attackers in a turn, however your Defence is lowered by 1 for every attacker after the first.

At any point before you take an action in a turn, you may choose to give up your regular action in order to "Dodge". When you choose to Dodge, you double your Defence total and roll this as a dice pool - subtracting your successes from an attacker's successes. When facing multiple attackers, reduce your Defence total by 1 before you double it. Dramatic Failures on a dodge roll reduce your defence total by 1 for the following turn.

Some exceptions to this rule include Merits such as the "Defensive Combat" Merit which allows you to apply either your Brawl or Weaponry dots to Defence in place of your Athletics, and various Fighting Style Merits such as the first level of "Street Fighting", which allows you to take a one die penalty to attack to calculate Defence using the higher rather than lower of Wits and Dex.

Defense = Lowest of Dexterity or Wits + Athletics + Celerity

answers.q17: ok... bestial triad is a Predatory Aura mechanic when two vamps clash.   You pick an aspect to clash with.   - Monster(Str|Bestial Condition)    - Seduce(Presence|Wanton Condition)    - Competive(Intelligence|Competitive Condition).   The target can either ACCEPT or REJECT the clash.   ACCEPTING and clashing will cost willpower from the responder. Test is BP+ attirbute + Aura Modifier.  Disciplines dont add to the check.  Winner gets +2 bonus vs loser for the scene Aura Modifier based on how hungry you are and if youre on your own feedning grounds.  REJECTING the clash is a big loss of face and doesnt guarantee you are not pursued. You dont need to spend the willpower though. The downside is you straight-up get the condition imposed by their challenge.
